name: gold-fresh-110-2026-06-05
description: Fresh generality suite — new analyst phrasing across all shapes + all tables + non-data routes (chat/knowledge/tool). Gold executed-validated.

Scenarios.Question: Break down our ticket volume by the region each one belongs to, and put the region's English name next to its total.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT r.NameEn, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN Regions r ON t.RegionId = r.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY r.NameEn ORDER BY TicketCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Break down payment counts by the method that was used, labelling each one with the payment method's name.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT pm.NameEn, COUNT(*) AS PaymentCount FROM Payments p JOIN PaymentMethods pm ON p.PaymentMethodId = pm.Id GROUP BY pm.NameEn ORDER BY PaymentCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Roll up the total amount paid under each payment method, tagging every figure with the method's name.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT pm.NameEn, SUM(p.Amount) AS TotalPaid FROM Payments p JOIN PaymentMethods pm ON p.PaymentMethodId = pm.Id GROUP BY pm.NameEn ORDER BY TotalPaid DESC;

Scenarios.Question: I want our outages split out by the type of service they hit, with each service type's name shown beside its outage tally.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT st.NameEn, COUNT(*) AS OutageCount FROM Outages o JOIN ServiceTypes st ON o.ServiceTypeId = st.Id GROUP BY st.NameEn ORDER BY OutageCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: I'd like to see total billed amounts grouped by which customer segment the bill's subsidy falls under, each one named.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT cs.NameEn, SUM(s.Amount) AS TotalSubsidy FROM Subsidies s JOIN CustomerSegments cs ON s.CustomerSegmentId = cs.Id GROUP BY cs.NameEn ORDER BY TotalSubsidy DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Break out the work-order count per department, with the owning department's English name attached to each.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT d.NameEn, COUNT(*) AS WorkOrderCount FROM WorkOrders w JOIN Departments d ON w.DepartmentId = d.Id GROUP BY d.NameEn ORDER BY WorkOrderCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Point me to the service types that have suffered 5 or more outages, each one named with its outage count.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT st.NameEn, COUNT(*) AS OutageCount FROM Outages o JOIN ServiceTypes st ON o.ServiceTypeId = st.Id GROUP BY st.NameEn HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5 ORDER BY OutageCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Which payment methods have processed more than 100 transactions? Name them with their counts.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT pm.NameEn, COUNT(*) AS PaymentCount FROM Payments p JOIN PaymentMethods pm ON p.PaymentMethodId = pm.Id GROUP BY pm.NameEn HAVING COUNT(*) > 100 ORDER BY PaymentCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Are there any regions that have never had a single outage logged against them? Name them.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT r.NameEn FROM Regions r WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Outages o WHERE o.RegionId = r.Id) ORDER BY r.NameEn;

Scenarios.Question: I'm trying to figure out which service types we've never set a tariff for. Surface them by name.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT st.NameEn FROM ServiceTypes st WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tariffs ta WHERE ta.ServiceTypeId = st.Id) ORDER BY st.NameEn;

Scenarios.Question: Walk me through any customers that have a bill on file but no payment ever recorded against it.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT DISTINCT c.FullNameEn FROM Customers c JOIN Bills b ON b.CustomerId = c.Id WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Payments p WHERE p.BillId = b.Id) ORDER BY c.FullNameEn;

Scenarios.Question: Where do we stand on departments that have never had a single work order assigned to them — how many such departments are there?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS DepartmentsWithNoWorkOrders FROM Departments d WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM WorkOrders w WHERE w.DepartmentId = d.Id);

Scenarios.Question: Single out the one region carrying the heaviest ticket load, and tell me its name and how many.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 1 r.NameEn, COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets t JOIN Regions r ON t.RegionId = r.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY r.NameEn ORDER BY TicketCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Which payment method comes out on top by number of transactions? Just the busiest one, named.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 1 pm.NameEn, COUNT(*) AS PaymentCount FROM Payments p JOIN PaymentMethods pm ON p.PaymentMethodId = pm.Id GROUP BY pm.NameEn ORDER BY PaymentCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Rank our regions by outage count and hand me just the top three, each named.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 3 r.NameEn, COUNT(*) AS OutageCount FROM Outages o JOIN Regions r ON o.RegionId = r.Id GROUP BY r.NameEn ORDER BY OutageCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Two things on outages: how many were flagged critical severity, and which single one hit the most customers?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Outages WHERE Severity = 'Critical') AS CriticalOutageCount, (SELECT TOP 1 OutageNumber FROM Outages ORDER BY AffectedCustomerCount DESC) AS WorstByAffectedCustomers;

Scenarios.Question: Give me two numbers on work orders side by side: the count that reached Completed, and the average affected-customers expectation across nothing — actually just the average duration in minutes from creation to completion for the completed ones.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS CompletedWorkOrders, AVG(DATEDIFF(MINUTE, CreatedAt, CompletedAt)) AS AvgMinutesToComplete FROM WorkOrders WHERE Status = 'Completed' AND CompletedAt IS NOT NULL;

Scenarios.Question: For outages that kicked off during 2025, break them out by service type name with a count for each.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT st.NameEn, COUNT(*) AS OutageCount FROM Outages o JOIN ServiceTypes st ON o.ServiceTypeId = st.Id WHERE o.StartedAt >= '2025-01-01' AND o.StartedAt < '2026-01-01' GROUP BY st.NameEn ORDER BY OutageCount DESC;

Scenarios.Question: Pull together payments settled in 2025 and total their amount per payment method, each method named.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT pm.NameEn, SUM(p.Amount) AS TotalPaid FROM Payments p JOIN PaymentMethods pm ON p.PaymentMethodId = pm.Id WHERE p.PaidAt >= '2025-01-01' AND p.PaidAt < '2026-01-01' GROUP BY pm.NameEn ORDER BY TotalPaid DESC;

Scenarios.Question: I'm curious — across the whole estate, how do our customers split out by their account standing?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT Status, COUNT(*) AS CustomerCount FROM Customers GROUP BY Status;

Scenarios.Question: I'd like a read on every work order that's still sitting in the Assigned bucket.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT OrderNumber, OrderType, Status FROM WorkOrders WHERE Status = 'Assigned';

Scenarios.Question: Tally up the total billed value sitting in our Overdue invoices right now.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT SUM(TotalAmount) AS OverdueTotal FROM Bills WHERE Status = 'Overdue';

Scenarios.Question: What does a typical payment land at, value-wise, once it's been Completed?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT AVG(Amount) AS AvgCompletedPayment FROM Payments WHERE Status = 'Completed';

Scenarios.Question: Break down our outages by how bad they were — I want the headcount per severity band.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT Severity, COUNT(*) AS OutageCount FROM Outages GROUP BY Severity;

Scenarios.Question: Of all the consumption figures our meters have logged, what's the single heaviest reading on record?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT MAX(Consumption) AS HeaviestConsumption FROM MeterReadings;

Scenarios.Question: I'm after the gentlest CSAT score anyone has ever handed us — the rock-bottom one.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT MIN(Score) AS LowestCsatScore FROM CsatResponses;

Scenarios.Question: How busy were we on the ticket front during the back half of 2025 — say, October through December?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS TicketCount FROM Tickets WHERE CreatedAt >= '2025-10-01' AND CreatedAt < '2026-01-01';

Scenarios.Question: Run me through the English names of every region we currently serve.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT NameEn FROM Regions;

Scenarios.Question: Roughly where do we stand on technician headcount that's still on the active roster?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS ActiveTechnicians FROM Technicians WHERE IsActive = 1;

Scenarios.Question: I want eyes on the service accounts that have gone Suspended on us.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT AccountNumber, Status FROM ServiceAccounts WHERE Status = 'Suspended';

Scenarios.Question: Carve up our assets by what kind of standing they're in, and tell me the tally for each.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT Status, COUNT(*) AS AssetCount FROM Assets GROUP BY Status;

Scenarios.Question: On average, what kind of tax slice are our tariffs carrying these days?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT AVG(TaxPercent) AS AvgTaxPercent FROM Tariffs;

Scenarios.Question: Walk me through the English labels of the service types we've got on the books.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT NameEn FROM ServiceTypes;

Scenarios.Question: Where does our department footprint sit if I only care about the ones still switched on?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS ActiveDepartments FROM Departments WHERE IsActive = 1;

Scenarios.Question: Add up the affected-customer figures across all the outages we've logged.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT SUM(AffectedCustomerCount) AS TotalAffectedCustomers FROM Outages;

Scenarios.Question: Morning! Hope your coffee kicked in already — mine's still working on it.
Honestly you've been a lifesaver this week — figured I'd say thanks before I dive back in.
Quick one off the books — what's a euro going for in dollars at the moment?
Got anything on whether Italy's got a bank holiday landing soon?
When something goes dark on the grid, what bits of info does the system actually hold against that outage record?
I keep mixing these up — where's the line between a tariff and one of its tiers in here?
Run me through the states a payment can land in — what are the valid options on that field?
Before I lean on you too hard — what sort of stuff is genuinely within your wheelhouse to answer?
